feedback.text: Hello dear EU, I fully agree with what your initiative stands for. For the dark patterns of videogames I would like to show some examples, first is "gacha games", these games are based on what is essentially known as gambling, there are many of them such as "Genshin Impact". What makes them so dangerous is that they sell themselves as free-to-play with some payment in-game, meaning kids have easy access to said games. There are already some cases in which kids have stolen the parents credit card to pay for said gacha games, so we have to stop this from happening anymore. I think the best way to approach this is to highly regulate them or, like Belgium, ban them entirely. The second one is the famous, maybe the word infamous would be more appropriate, game "The Sims 4", a game by EA which was released at launch unfinished. The game instead of having the simple basic of what a life simulation should have, like the games that preceded it, have been sealed behind packs or kits; if you want the full game with all features you have to pay up to more than 1200. It also added what I considered a "battle pass", rewards given to you if spend a lot of time in the videogame, which could create an harmful addiction. Even in this game, we should have a high regulation on what can be sold, because EA has demonstrated time and time again that it will make consumers pay even for something small. Now I would like to talk about the Stop Destroying Videogames initiative and the Visa/Mastercard censorship. While I was not personally affected, the fact that many people who payed for a game have seen it die because companies didn't plan for extended playing, is something that should be stopped. Me, as many other people, would like to add another clause to said initiative, which protects videogames to be destroyed completely. For the second point, the Visa/Mastercard censorship, we have to look at an ex EU country, which is the UK. Visa/Mastercard have censored some games and other sites on the name of "protecting children", these protection have already censored some political messages (fair criticism against the government), we have to prevent this from happening inside the EU. So dear EU, here is the summary of my feedback: Have some regulations against gambling, such as gacha games Prevent companies to make consumers pay for anything, even small changes of said game. Add a clause that prevents videogames from being destroyed forever (this can be found in the Destroying Videogames Initiative) Stop payment processor to be able to censor news, criticism and/or videos. I hope that all the feedbacks you're receiving will help the consumer have more rights. Thank you dear EU.
